message:
feat(sources)+perf(indexer): persisted disabled_agents + archive-fingerprint-keyed skip of redundant full-scan rebuilds

Two related changes landing together because they share the
FrankenStorage/indexer seams and are validated end-to-end by the same
full-index e2e suite.

== 1. feat(sources): persisted `disabled_agents` with CLI + archive purge ==

Users previously had no first-class way to tell cass "don't index
OpenClaw sessions even though the files are on disk" short of moving
the source files out of the way. Add a persisted exclusion list with a
real CLI surface and a local-archive purge path.

src/sources/config.rs
- Add `disabled_agents: Vec<String>` on `SourcesConfig` (default empty,
  skip_serializing_if = Vec::is_empty so clean installs keep a minimal
  sources.toml).
- New helpers:
  * `is_agent_disabled(&self, agent) -> bool`
  * `exclude_agent_from_indexing(&mut self, agent) -> Result<bool>`
  * `include_agent_in_indexing(&mut self, agent) -> Result<bool>`
  * `configured_disabled_agents() -> Vec<String>` (sorted, deduped)
- Validation: `disabled_agents[i]` cannot be empty/whitespace-only.
- Alias normalization (`normalize_agent_config_name`): collapses
  `claude-code` → `claude` so users can type any known spelling and
  still persist the internal connector slug. Hyphens/underscores and
  leading/trailing whitespace are normalized in `agent_name_key`
  (promoted to `pub(crate)` so the indexer can reuse the same key).
- Unit tests cover: exclude+include normalize+dedup, alias collapse,
  validation of empty entries, TOML round-trip preserving exclusions.

src/lib.rs (CLI)
- New `SourcesCommand::Agents(AgentsAction)` subcommand tree:
  * `cass sources agents list` (with `--json` / `--robot` aliases)
  * `cass sources agents exclude <agent> [--keep-indexed-data]`
  * `cass sources agents include <agent>`
- `run_agents_command` wires each action. `exclude` by default also
  calls `purge_excluded_agent_archive_data` which opens the local
  FrankenStorage, deletes conversations + messages for the agent,
  rebuilds FTS, and rebuilds analytics. `--keep-indexed-data` persists
  the exclusion but leaves the archive intact.
- `archive_agent_slug_for_exclusion` maps user-facing aliases
  (`claude` / `claude-code` / `claude_code`) to the archive-side slug
  (`claude_code`) so purges hit the right row.
- `run_sources_list` now surfaces `disabled_agents` in both the
  structured JSON output (top-level key alongside `sources`/`total`)
  and the human-readable output ("Excluded agents: …" line; friendly
  note when there are no sources but exclusions are still active).
- `is_robot_mode` recognises the new `Agents::List { json }` arm.
- `print_robot_docs(RobotTopic::Sources)` gains a dedicated section
  documenting the new commands, the on-disk TOML
  (`disabled_agents = [...]`), and the `--keep-indexed-data` flag.

src/indexer/mod.rs
- New `configured_connector_factories()` returning the canonical list
  of connector name→factory pairs; `filter_disabled_connector_factories`
  removes any whose normalized slug matches `SourcesConfig.is_agent_disabled`.
- `run_batch_index_with_connector_factories(...)` takes a filtered
  factory set so both local scans and remote-mirror scans honour the
  exclusion list instead of hard-coding the full factory map.
- Unit test `configured_connector_factories_skip_disabled_agents_from_sources_config`
  proves the filter drops the right entry when `disabled_agents`
  contains a matching slug (including through alias normalization).

src/storage/sqlite.rs
- `AgentArchivePurgeResult { conversations_deleted, messages_deleted }`.
- `purge_agent_archive_data(agent_slug)`: looks up the agent row, counts
  conversations + messages to delete, then deletes them in a single
  transaction. Returns zero-valued result (no-op) when the agent row
  doesn't exist, so the CLI stays idempotent.
- Support helpers `total_conversation_count()` and `total_message_count()`
  for reporting / fingerprinting.

src/ui/app.rs
- Existing unit tests that construct literal `SourcesConfig { sources: … }`
  are updated to also set `disabled_agents: vec![]` now that the struct
  has a new field. No behavior change.

tests/e2e_sources.rs
- New `seed_archive_conversation` helper drops a realistic
  agent+conversation+messages row into the local FrankenStorage so the
  purge path has something to delete.
- Three new e2e tests, all going through the real `cass` binary:
  * `sources_agents_exclude_and_list_json`
  * `sources_agents_include_removes_existing_exclusion`
  * `sources_agents_exclude_purges_local_archive_data_by_default`

== 2. perf(indexer): skip rebuilds when archive fingerprint is unchanged ==

Three heavy post-full-scan rebuild paths were firing on every `cass
index --full` even when the underlying archive had not changed at all,
wasting minutes on large installs. Gate each on an archive fingerprint
recorded in `meta` and short-circuit when it matches.

src/storage/sqlite.rs — fingerprint meta keys:
- `fts_frankensqlite_archive_fingerprint` (FTS rebuild freshness)
- `daily_stats_archive_fingerprint` (daily-stats freshness)
- `daily_stats_health_generation` (schema version for the above)
- Helper pairs:
  * `fallback_fts_is_known_healthy_for_archive_fingerprint(fp)` +
    `record_search_fallback_fts_archive_fingerprint(fp)`
  * `daily_stats_is_known_healthy_for_archive_fingerprint(fp)` +
    `record_daily_stats_archive_fingerprint(fp)`
  * `read_fts_franken_rebuild_archive_fingerprint()` + `read_daily_stats_archive_fingerprint()`
- `rebuild_token_daily_stats()` now returns the fresh row count so
  callers can report `rows_created` and treat zero rows as
  `AlreadyHealthy` without a double-probe.

src/indexer/mod.rs — gating logic + predicate extraction:
- New outcome enums `FallbackFtsRepairOutcome::{ SkippedKnownHealthyForFingerprint, Repaired }`
  and `DailyStatsRepairOutcome::{ SkippedKnownHealthyForFingerprint, AlreadyHealthy, Rebuilt { rows_created, total_sessions } }`
  so the caller can log precisely what happened and avoid redundant
  probes on the "already healthy" branch.
- `repair_fallback_fts_after_full_index_run` and
  `repair_daily_stats_if_drifted` now accept the known archive
  fingerprint and return `SkippedKnownHealthyForFingerprint` when the
  archive-side fingerprint matches, otherwise they do the real repair
  and record the new fingerprint.
- Lexical rebuild state (`.lexical-rebuild-state.json`):
  `MatchingLexicalRebuildStateStatus` now also carries
  `completed_exact_totals` and `completed_storage_fingerprint`, and
  `should_preserve_matching_completed_lexical_checkpoint_during_full_scan`
  keeps the existing checkpoint instead of deleting-and-recreating it
  across plain no-op full scans.
- New predicates promoted to named functions (easier to unit test and
  harder to get wrong than inline boolean chains):
  * `lexical_population_strategy_requires_inline_tantivy`
  * `should_preflight_existing_tantivy_reader`
  * `should_probe_live_tantivy_docs_for_post_full_scan_skip` +
    `observed_tantivy_docs_for_post_full_scan_skip`
  * `should_force_authoritative_rebuild` (renamed its input from
    `storage_rebuilt` → `canonical_storage_rebuilt` to make it clear
    it gates on canonical, not derived, storage changes)
  * `should_repair_daily_stats_after_historical_salvage`
  * `repair_daily_stats_if_drifted` (wraps the outcome enum path)
- `should_skip_noop_final_lexical_checkpoint_refresh` and
  `should_skip_post_full_scan_authoritative_rebuild` take their
  `MatchingLexicalRebuildStateStatus` by reference now (it grew fields
  that made Copy unsound).
- Unit tests land alongside:
  * `streaming_consumer_can_defer_authoritative_lexical_updates_without_tantivy_writer`
  * `batch_index_can_defer_authoritative_lexical_updates_without_tantivy_writer`
  * `repair_daily_stats_if_drifted_skips_known_healthy_archive_fingerprint`
  * `daily_stats_repair_after_historical_salvage_avoids_duplicate_plain_full_probe`
  * `full_run_fallback_fts_repair_skips_known_healthy_archive_fingerprint`
  * `preserve_matching_completed_lexical_checkpoint_during_full_scan_requires_plain_full_scan_with_checkpoint`
  * `preflight_existing_tantivy_reader_is_skipped_for_resume_or_full_rebuilds`
  * `live_tantivy_doc_probe_for_post_full_skip_only_runs_for_noop_full_candidates`
  * `authoritative_rebuild_requirement_only_treats_canonical_storage_reset_as_rebuild`

tests/cli_index.rs
- `repeat_full_json_preserves_exact_totals_when_noop_scan_underreports`
  now also snapshots the mtime of `.lexical-rebuild-state.json` before
  the second run, waits 5ms to make any accidental rewrite detectable,
  and asserts the mtime is identical afterwards — proving the
  preservation path actually keeps the file instead of recreating it.

Net effect on a large local archive where the second `cass index
--full` is a no-op: fallback FTS rebuild, daily-stats rebuild, and
lexical checkpoint recreation are all skipped, and the existing
`.lexical-rebuild-state.json` survives byte-for-byte.
